📉 SOXX −11.24% · Lead Scout Confirmed Breakdown
§1 — SOXX Lead Scout Chart

SOXX −11.24% on Friday — The Lead Scout Has Spoken

SOXX is Dr. UV's Lead Scout for the broader market. When SOXX breaks down hard, the rest of the market follows within days to weeks. Friday's −11.24% single-session collapse is one of the largest single-day drops on record. The UVCMI 777™ had been warning across all timeframes for weeks. The Lead Scout has now confirmed the breakdown.

SOXX — THE LEAD SCOUT

PHLX SEMICONDUCTOR INDEX ETF · DR. UV's PRIMARY MARKET SCOUT

SOXX represents the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index — the most forward-looking sector in the S&P universe. Semiconductors lead because they feed every technology cycle: AI, data centers, smartphones, EVs, defense. When SOXX leads down, broad market follows.

Friday's collapse of −11.24% in a single session confirms what the UVCMI 777™ B-Screen, ST, and IT-LT screens were signaling for weeks: the rotation out of semiconductor leadership has turned into a full rout. YTD B1 reading of +7,765 means 7+ years of price momentum is now being unwound in real time.

Lead Scout Signal: SOXX closing −11.24% on above-average volume while YTD B1 remains at +7,765 is a classic distribution signal. The strongest performers of the cycle are being sold hardest. This is not a dip to buy — this is the Lead Scout confirming a regime change.

Context: The NYSE McClellan Oscillator divergence (documented in Post #571) crossed below zero exactly as SOXX price collapsed. Breadth and price have now aligned to the downside simultaneously.

§2 — B Screen · SOXX Deep Dive

SOXX B-Screen — All Timeframes at a Glance

The B Screen shows SOXX across all B-Screen timeframes: B1 Year/Quarter/Month/Week/Day/2H/1H. The collapse across every timeframe simultaneously is the signature of a true trend change, not a temporary pullback.

Dr. UV's SOXX · B Screen · B1 Y/Q/M/W/D/2H/1H · All timeframes deteriorating simultaneously · June 5, 2026 close

B-Screen Reading — What You Are Seeing

The B-Screen (Breadth Screen) shows momentum across multiple timeframes simultaneously using the UVCMI 777™ formula. Green bars indicate positive momentum; red bars indicate negative. When all timeframes turn red simultaneously, it confirms a genuine trend reversal rather than a timeframe-specific pullback.

SOXX's B-Screen showing multi-timeframe deterioration while XLP, XLU, IDU, and XLRE remain green is the definitive visual confirmation of defensive rotation. The Lead Scout has confirmed: risk-off is now the dominant regime.

§3 — ST Screen · Short-Term

SOXX Short-Term Momentum — UVCMI 1H / 2H / 4H

The ST Screen covers the short-term timeframes: UVCMI 1H, 2H, and 4H. Friday's collapse registered a SOXX 1H reading that ranks among the most extreme negative readings in the UVCMI database.

Dr. UV's SOXX · ST Screen · UVCMI 1H/2H/4H · SOXX 1H: extreme negative · Orange crossing lines mark momentum reversal · June 5, 2026

ST Screen Reading — Short-Term Regime

The 1H UVCMI reading for SOXX on June 5 clocked at −723 — one of the most extreme short-term momentum readings recorded. For context, QQQ 1H was −442 and XSD 1H was −743. The semiconductor complex is being hit harder than any other sector on every timeframe.

Short-term momentum of this magnitude typically takes 3–7 sessions to partially revert. However, given the IT-LT confirmation (see §4), a quick bounce would be a selling opportunity, not a signal to re-enter longs in the semiconductor space.

§4 — IT-LT Screen · Intermediate & Long-Term

SOXX Intermediate & Long-Term Momentum — UVCMI D / 2D / 3D / W

The IT-LT Screen covers the intermediate and long-term timeframes: UVCMI Daily, 2-Day, 3-Day, and Weekly. This is where the real structural signal lives. A weekly timeframe UVCMI negative reading after a YTD B1 of +7,765 is the most bearish possible combination.

Dr. UV's SOXX · IT-LT Screen · UVCMI D/2D/3D/W · XSD W: massive positive being consumed · Defensive sectors still holding W · June 5, 2026

IT-LT Screen Reading — Structural Confirmation

The IT-LT screen is the most important for strategic positioning. The weekly timeframe UVCMI for SOXX has now flipped negative after being deeply positive for the entire YTD run. This is not a short-term noise event — this is the structural momentum shift that the UVCMI 777™ was designed to identify before it becomes obvious on price charts.

Compare SOXX's IT-LT screen to XLP (Consumer Staples) and XLU (Utilities) — both remain in positive territory on the weekly timeframe. The divergence between semiconductor (Lead Scout) and defensive sectors on the IT-LT screen is the clearest signal of a full sector rotation in progress.

§5 — UVCMI 20/20 Vision™ · SOXX

SOXX 20/20 Vision™ — 20 Simultaneous Timeframes

The SOXX UVCMI 20/20 Vision™ screen extends the analysis to 20 simultaneous timeframes. This is Dr. UV's broadest diagnostic view — when a Lead Scout like SOXX shows deterioration across all 20 timeframes, the signal strength is at maximum.

UVCMI 20/20 Vision™ — SOXX

20 TIMEFRAMES · SOXX LEAD SCOUT · PROPRIETARY DR. UV VIEW

The 20/20 Vision™ screen is the most comprehensive single-ticker diagnostic available in the UVCMI 777™ system. By running all 20 timeframes simultaneously on SOXX, Dr. UV can identify exactly where in the momentum cycle the Lead Scout sits — early deterioration, mid-collapse, or exhaustion.

The orange X crossing patterns visible in today's screen indicate simultaneous timeframe reversals. Multiple X formations in a single session on the Lead Scout means the breakdown is not limited to any one timeframe — it is systemic across the entire SOXX momentum profile. This is the highest-confidence bearish signal the UVCMI 777™ system can generate for a single ticker.

Dr. UV's SOXX · UVCMI 20/20 Vision™ · 20 Simultaneous Timeframes · Multiple orange X crossing patterns across all timeframes · Full momentum collapse confirmed · June 5, 2026

20/20 Vision™ Signal: When the SOXX Lead Scout shows X patterns across all 20 timeframes simultaneously, it means every timeframe has crossed from bull to bear in a single session. This happens approximately 2–3 times per decade. The last comparable reading preceded a multi-month market correction.

§6 — All UVCMI 777™ · Full Sector View

SOXX in Context — Full UVCMI 777™ Cross-Sector Snapshot

The All UVCMI 777™ screen places SOXX in its full sector context. This view shows all tracked tickers across all timeframes simultaneously, with orange X patterns marking the sectors experiencing simultaneous momentum reversals on June 5, 2026.

Dr. UV's UVCMI 777™ All Timeframes · Full cross-sector view · Orange X patterns mark simultaneous sector reversals · SOXX as Lead Scout at the forefront of the breakdown · SPY highlighted blue (center) · June 5, 2026

Cross-Sector Context — SOXX vs. the Field

The All UVCMI 777™ view confirms what the SOXX-specific screens showed: this is not an isolated semiconductor event. The orange X patterns propagate outward from SOXX (the Lead Scout) through XSD, XLK, XNTK, and XITK before reaching the broad market (SPY, highlighted in blue). Defensive sectors — XLP, XLU, IDU, XLRE — show green or flat readings, confirming the rotation thesis.

The architecture of this screen proves Dr. UV's Lead Scout framework: SOXX moves first, the tech complex moves second, the broad market (SPY) moves third, and defensive sectors reprice last. Friday's session showed all four stages occurring simultaneously, which means the rotation is not early-stage — it is in full acceleration.

UVCMI 777™ · UVCMI 20/20 Vision™ · DR UVC Market Insights™
DR. UV's SOXX Lead Scout Tracker™ · B-Screen™ · RelS vs SPY™ · DR UVC Universal™
UVCMI 777™ is Dr. UV's Master Indicator calculated from a single proprietary formula incorporating the last 60 units of closing prices. Runs across 7 simultaneous timeframes (1H, 2H, 4H, D, 2D, 3D, W). UVCMI 20/20 Vision™ extends this to 20 simultaneous timeframes for broad diagnostic purposes. Green = positive momentum · Red = negative momentum. SOXX Lead Scout™ is Dr. UV's framework for using SOXX as the primary leading indicator for broad market direction.
